Amplia Therapeutics ( (AU:ATX) ) just unveiled an update.

Amplia Therapeutics Limited has released its corporate governance statement for the financial year ending March 31, 2025, which is available on their website. The statement outlines the company’s adherence to the ASX Corporate Governance Council’s principles and recommendations, detailing the roles and responsibilities of its board and management. This disclosure is crucial for stakeholders as it confirms the company’s commitment to transparency and accountability in its governance practices.

More about Amplia Therapeutics

Amplia Therapeutics Limited operates in the biotechnology industry, focusing on developing therapeutics for the treatment of cancer and fibrotic diseases. The company is committed to advancing its drug candidates through clinical trials to address unmet medical needs.

data.title: EP&T Global Announces Change in Joint Company Secretary
data.description:
Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

The latest update is out from EP&T Global Ltd. ( (AU:EPX) ).

EP&T Global Limited has announced a change in its joint Company Secretary position, with Olga Smejkalova resigning and Andrew Kabega taking over the role. This change, effective from May 29, 2025, also designates Kabega as the primary contact for ASX communications. This strategic personnel change is expected to streamline communications with the ASX, potentially impacting the company’s operational efficiency and stakeholder engagement.

More about EP&T Global Ltd.

EP&T Global is a leading provider of building efficiency platforms, specializing in data as a service to enhance cost and energy efficiency in buildings. Their EDGE cloud technology is renowned for reducing energy costs and greenhouse gas emissions in commercial real estate by analyzing vast amounts of data to identify inefficiencies and opportunities for CO2e reduction.

data.title: Australian Building Permits Plunge: Stock Market Impact?
data.description:
In a recent economic update from Australia, the Building Permits data for April was released, revealing a significant decline. The figures showed a decrease of 5.7%, falling short of the anticipated 3.1% increase. This comes after a previous drop of 7.1%, indicating a continuing downward trend in the construction sector.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

This unexpected decline in building permits could have notable implications for the Australian stock market, particularly affecting construction and real estate stocks. Investors might perceive this as a signal of a slowing economy, potentially leading to cautious trading and a reevaluation of growth prospects in these sectors. The broader market could experience volatility as traders digest the implications of a weaker construction outlook, impacting investor sentiment and market dynamics.

data.title: Surprise Decline in Pending Home Sales Shakes Market
data.description:
Today, the latest figures for Pending Home Sales Year-over-Year (YoY) in April were released, revealing a significant decline. The actual data showed a drop of 2.5%, falling short of the anticipated 1.9% increase. This marks a considerable downturn from the previous month’s figure of -0.6%, indicating a deeper contraction in the housing market than expected.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

The unexpected decline in pending home sales could have ripple effects across the stock market, particularly impacting sectors tied to real estate and construction. Investors may become cautious, leading to potential sell-offs in stocks related to homebuilding companies, mortgage lenders, and real estate investment trusts (REITs). Additionally, this downturn might prompt concerns about consumer confidence and spending, which could influence broader market sentiment. As the housing market often reflects the overall economic health, this data might lead to increased volatility in the coming days as investors reassess their strategies.

Inca Minerals Limited ( (AU:ICG) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Inca Minerals Limited has announced a General Meeting of shareholders scheduled for July 1, 2025, at their West Perth location. The meeting will be held physically, and shareholders are encouraged to vote via proxy in advance. The company has opted not to send hard copies of the Notice of Meeting, instead making it available online. This move reflects a focus on digital accessibility and efficiency, potentially impacting shareholder engagement and participation.

Inca Minerals Limited is a company operating in the mining industry, focusing on exploration and development of mineral resources. The company is listed on the Australian Securities Exchange under the code ASX: ICG.

The latest announcement is out from Critical Resources Ltd ( (AU:CRR) ).

Critical Resources Ltd announced that all resolutions proposed at its Annual General Meeting were passed, indicating strong shareholder support for the company’s strategic direction. This development is likely to bolster the company’s operations and enhance its positioning in the critical metals industry, potentially benefiting stakeholders by ensuring continued progress in its key projects.

Critical Resources Ltd is engaged in the exploration, development, and delivery of critical metals essential for a decarbonized future. The company is focused on its Mavis Lake Lithium Project in Ontario, Canada, and the Hall Peak Base Metals Project in New South Wales, Australia. data.description:
Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

City Developments ( (SG:C09) ) has issued an update.

CDI reported an increased pre-tax profit of $26.8 million for 2024, driven by favorable market conditions and strategic project approvals. The company is committed to sustainability and has expanded its landholdings by 39% over five years. The retirement of Independent Director John Henderson marks the end of an era, as he played a crucial role in guiding the company through significant developments and challenges.

The most recent analyst rating on (SG:C09) stock is a Hold with a S$4.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on City Developments stock, see the SG:C09 Stock Forecast page.

More about City Developments

City Developments (CDI) operates in the real estate industry, focusing on residential and mixed-use developments. The company is actively expanding its landholdings and development portfolio, with significant projects in areas like Hamilton, Nelson, and Christchurch.
